messages.content:
Here's how you can narrate "Five Little Monkeys" from the provided document:

Start by swinging your arms like the monkeys are swinging on a tree, with fingers extended. As you sing "Fingers extended swing your hand back and forth", move your hands in a back-and-forth motion.

Next, mimic teasing Mr. Alligator by shaking your head and wagging your finger like you're saying "no, no" while singing "Teasing Mr. Alligator, 'can't catch me, can't catch me'".

Now, quietly whisper "Along came Mr. Alligator quiet as can be" and make a SSSHHH motion with your hand.

Finally, mimic the sound of Mr. Alligator's mouth snapping shut by clapping your hands together while singing "SNAP! Clap hands together like the mouth of an alligator".

Continue to count down from five little monkeys swinging on a tree, each time removing one monkey until only one is left.
what is the story of the three blind mice?

The story of the Three Blind Mice goes like this:

Three blind mice were running around, and they all ran after the farmer's wife. In her anger or attempt to chase them away, she cut off their tails with a carving knife. The nursery rhyme ends with the question "Did ever see such a sight in your life?" implying that seeing three blind mice was an unusual and amusing thing.
what is the story of the rhymes hush little baby?

The story of "Hush Little Baby" is about a child who is being reassured by their mother that they will be taken care of, no matter what happens. The song lists various hypothetical scenarios where things might go wrong (the mockingbird won't sing, the diamond ring turns brass, etc.), but each time, Mama promises to buy something new for her baby until eventually, she says "You'll still be the sweetest little boy in town." This suggests that even if all these things were to happen, the child's value and worth would remain unchanged. The song is a lullaby meant to comfort and soothe the baby, telling them that they are loved and will always have their parents' care.
